Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Fixfest is a global gathering of repairers, tinkerers, and activists 104 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 207 upvotes, 30 comments
  3. Algebraic topology: knots links and braids 49 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. An opinionated take on how to do important research that matters 36 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 117 upvotes, 26 comments
  6. Fontcrafter: Turn Your Handwriting into a Real Font 376 upvotes, 125 comments
  7. Workers report watching Ray-Ban Meta-shot footage of people using the bathroom 47 upvotes, 10 comments
  8. FFmpeg at Meta: Media Processing at Scale 199 upvotes, 83 comments
  9. Show HN: The Mog Programming Language 61 upvotes, 20 comments
  10. FreeBSD Capsicum vs. Linux Seccomp Process Sandboxing 93 upvotes, 36 comments
  11. Reverse-engineering the UniFi inform protocol 122 upvotes, 50 comments
  12. Rethinking Syntax: Binding by Adjacency 21 upvotes, 5 comments
  13. Flash media longevity testing – 6 years later 101 upvotes, 47 comments
  14. Uber reported to the state that I was fired for "annoying a coworker." 59 upvotes, 25 comments
  15. Ireland shuts last coal plant, becomes 15th coal-free country in Europe (2025) 744 upvotes, 455 comments
  16. Restoring a Sun SPARCstation IPX part 1: PSU and NVRAM (2020) 71 upvotes, 37 comments
  17. Jolla on track to ship new phone with Sailfish OS, user-replaceable battery 129 upvotes, 86 comments
  18. US Court of Appeals: TOS may be updated by email, use can imply consent [pdf] 493 upvotes, 383 comments
  19. Unlocking Python's Cores:Energy Implications of Removing the GIL 113 upvotes, 77 comments
  20. What I Always Wanted to Know about Second Class Values 18 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. The Most Beautiful Freezer in the World: Notes on Baking at the South Pole 8 upvotes, 2 comments
  22. Launch HN: Terminal Use (YC W26) – Vercel for filesystem-based agents 48 upvotes, 33 comments
  23. DARPA's new X-76 82 upvotes, 67 comments
  24. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 154 upvotes, 148 comments
  25. Durdraw – ANSI art editor for Unix-like systems 11 upvotes, 5 comments
  26. Bluesky CEO Jay Graber is stepping down 116 upvotes, 119 comments
  27. Velxio, Arduino Emulator 9 upvotes, 4 comments
  28. Show HN: DenchClaw – Local CRM on Top of OpenClaw 50 upvotes, 50 comments
  29. Grammarly is offering ‘expert’ AI reviews from famous dead and living writers 117 upvotes, 149 comments
  30. Florida judge rules red light camera tickets are unconstitutional 137 upvotes, 220 comments

Last generated 4:30am, 10 March 2026. Source here.
